name: Supabase REST API
what_it_does: Supabase is an open-source Firebase alternative built on PostgreSQL that auto-generates a RESTful API (via PostgREST) from your database schema. Every table and view immediately gets CRUD endpoints at your-project.supabase.co/rest/v1/. Supabase also provides a Management API for provisioning and managing projects programmatically, a Realtime API for subscribing to database changes, an Auth service (JWT-based), and an official MCP server for AI agent integration. The platform combines multiple services — database, auth, storage, edge functions, and realtime — behind a single project interface, making it particularly powerful for agents that need to manage the full application stack.
use_cases: Auto-generated REST CRUD API over PostgreSQL tables without writing any backend code
Agent-driven schema changes using the Supabase Management API (create tables, add columns, run migrations)
Real-time data subscriptions for agents monitoring live database changes via WebSocket
User authentication and session management using Supabase Auth with JWT verification
File storage operations via Supabase Storage with auto-generated signed URLs
Multi-project provisioning for multi-tenant SaaS using the Management API
not_for: Non-Supabase PostgreSQL databases (use direct pg drivers or PostgREST self-hosted instead)
Extremely high-throughput OLAP analytics (Supabase PostgreSQL is OLTP-optimized)
Teams requiring on-premise deployment without cloud connectivity
Applications needing sub-10ms latency without read replicas (Supabase has geo-routing but single region by default)
best_when: You want a complete backend platform (database + auth + storage + realtime + API) on top of PostgreSQL, with excellent developer tooling, an auto-generated REST API, and MCP server support for AI agent workflows.
avoid_when: You only need a PostgreSQL database without the platform features, or you're running on-premise without cloud access.

alternatives.reason: Neon is better for branching-focused workflows; Supabase has the richer full-stack platform
The MCP server variant — prefer supabase-api for REST API automation, supabase-mcp for agent chat integration
PlanetScale offers similar branching but is MySQL-only and lacks the full platform features

auth.notes: Three credential tiers: (1) anon key — public key for client-side use, respects Row Level Security policies; (2) service_role key — bypasses RLS, full database access, treat like root credentials; (3) Personal Access Token (PAT) for Management API. PostgREST auto-generates API with RLS enforcement when anon key is used. Agents using service_role key have unrestricted access — critical security consideration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 500MB database, 1GB storage, 50,000 monthly active users, 500K edge function invocations, 2 projects
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro $25/project/month (8GB database, 100GB storage, 100K MAU), Team $599/month (unlimited projects, HIPAA), Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free tier projects are paused after 1 week of inactivity — first request after pause has a cold start delay. Pro plan needed for production workloads. Database branching requires Pro plan. Point-in-time recovery available on Pro and above.

performance.rate_limits: PostgREST API: configurable via max_rows setting. Management API: not publicly documented. Realtime: concurrent connections limited by plan.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: PostgREST layer returns structured JSON error responses with code, message, details, and hint fields — very agent-friendly. SQL errors include PostgreSQL error codes. The Management API returns standard HTTP errors with descriptive messages. MCP server surfaces both API and SQL errors with clear context.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING/UPDATE provides true upsert semantics. PostgREST supports Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates and ignore-duplicates headers for idempotent writes. Migrations are tracked via schema_migrations table with timestamps. Management API project creation is not idempotent by name.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier projects pause after 1 week of inactivity — first request after pause triggers a 5-30 second cold start with 503 errors before resuming
service_role key bypasses ALL Row Level Security policies — agents using it have unrestricted read/write to all tables; use anon key with RLS for least-privilege agent access
PostgREST auto-generated API requires schema cache refresh (via NOTIFY pgrst, reload) when new tables are added — MCP server and API may return 404 for new tables until cache reloads
GraphQL API (pg_graphql extension) uses a separate endpoint (/graphql/v1) and requires understanding of the auto-generated schema
Storage file URLs are signed and expire — agents must handle URL refresh when working with stored files
Realtime subscriptions require explicit enable_realtime() and proper RLS policies — tables not enabled for realtime won't broadcast changes
Edge functions run on Deno, not Node.js — npm packages not directly compatible; use deno-compatible imports or esm.sh
Management API requires separate Personal Access Token from project API keys — different credential for infrastructure vs data plane
